channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Iran says won't reopen Hormuz as long as U.S. blockade remains
item.description: Iran seized two ships attempting to cross the Strait of Hormuz and has not decided on joining new US talks. Iran Guards say 'seized' two ships attempting to cross Strait of Hormuz; Iran says it has yet to decide whether to join a new round of talks with the U. S.

item.title: White House accuses Tehran of ‘acting like a bunch of pirates’ after attack on two ships
item.description: The White House accused Tehran of acting like pirates after Iran attacked two ships. Iran-US war latest: White House accuses Tehran of ‘acting like a bunch of pirates’ after attack on two ships The Independent.

item.title: US Navy chief out as Iran seizes 2 ships amid Hormuz blockade: Live updates
item.description: Iran seized two ships amid a Hormuz blockade and the US Navy chief is out. US Navy chief out as Iran seizes 2 ships amid Hormuz blockade: Live updates USA Today.

item.title: White House says Trump ‘satisfied’ with blockade after Iran says it seized two ships in strait of
item.description: Iran seized two ships in the Strait of Hormuz. The White House said Trump is satisfied with the blockade. Middle East crisis live: White House says Trump ‘satisfied’ with blockade after Iran says it seized two ships in strait of Hormuz The Guardian.

item.title: Why the US military is stuck using US$1 million missiles against Iran’s US$20,000 drones
item.description: The US military uses $1 million missiles against Iran's $20,000 drones due to bureaucracy. The US military doesn’t have a technology problem – it has a bureaucracy problem, says a former US Air Force officer.

item.title: Why emerging markets are dismissing bad Iran news
item.description: Emerging markets rebounded despite bad Iran news, showing value in the space. The fact that developing nations’ equities are able to rebound despite the bloc’s vulnerability to energy shocks shows there’s value in this space, says Shuli Ren for Bloomberg Opinion.

item.title: Three Ships Attacked in Strait of Hormuz, Escorting Two to Its Coast
item.description: Three ships were attacked in the Strait of Hormuz, with two escorted to the Iranian coast. The incident occurred in a vital waterway for global oil shipments. Authorities are investigating the attacks.

item.title: Interview – Rhys Machold - E-International Relations
item.description: Rhys Machold was interviewed by E-International Relations. Details of the interview are not specified. The interview is available on the E-International Relations website. Interview – Rhys Machold E-International Relations. Interview – Rhys Machold - E-International Relations.

item.title: Virtual Panel on United States-South Africa Relations: Ambassador Bozell and a Shifting Global
item.description: Ambassador Bozell discussed United States-South Africa relations at a virtual panel. Virtual Panel on United States-South Africa Relations: Ambassador Bozell and a Shifting Global Landscape Global Atlanta.

item.title: Turkiye making efforts to revive Russia-Ukraine talks, says Erdogan
item.description: Turkish President Erdogan stated that Türkiye is making efforts to revive Russia-Ukraine talks. Erdogan met with NATO chief as Kyiv asked Ankara to host a leaders' level meeting with Russia.

item.title: Iranian Forces Claim to Seize 2 Ships Near Strait of Hormuz After Trump Extends Truce
item.description: Iranian forces seized two ships near the Strait of Hormuz after Trump extended a truce. Iran War Live Updates: Iranian Forces Claim to Seize 2 Ships Near Strait of Hormuz After Trump Extends Truce The New York Times.

item.title: Israeli forces fire tear gas at funeral of Palestinians killed by settlers
item.description: Israeli forces fired tear gas at a funeral of two Palestinians killed by settlers in the occupied West Bank. The incident occurred at a school where the funeral was being held.

item.title: Trump calls for Republicans to unify around budget reconciliation push
item.description: Former US President Trump called for Republicans to unify around a budget reconciliation push. This would allow conservative lawmakers to bypass Democratic opposition in the Senate with a simple majority.

item.title: Trump calls Virginia election ‘rigged’ after redistricting referendum
item.description: Trump claimed the Virginia election was rigged after a slim majority of 51.4% voted in favour of redistricting. The measure passed with a narrow margin of 3.9 percentage points. Trump’s comments came after the Virginia measure passed by a slim margy of 51.4 in favour and 48.5 against.

item.title: Dutch intelligence assesses when Russia could move toward conflict with NATO - RBC-Ukraine
item.description: Dutch intelligence is assessing when Russia might move towards conflict with NATO. The assessment is not specified in the available information. Dutch intelligence assesses when Russia could move toward conflict with NATO RBC-Ukraine.

item.title: Iran attacks 3 ships in the Strait of Hormuz as Trump indefinitely extends ceasefire
item.description: Iran attacked three ships in the Strait of Hormuz as Trump extended a ceasefire indefinitely. Live updates: Iran attacks 3 ships in the Strait of Hormuz as Trump indefinitely extends ceasefire AP News.

item.title: 2 Journalists Injured In Israeli Airstrike In Lebanon
item.description: Two Al-Akhbar journalists were injured in an Israeli airstrike in Lebanon. The Lebanese daily newspaper Al-Akhbar said its two journalists were injured in Israeli strike. 2 Journalists Injured In Israeli Airstrike In Lebanon.

item.title: Putin Can Meet Zelensky Only To Agree Final Conflict Arrangements: Kremlin
item.description: Russian President Putin can only meet Ukrainian President Zelenskiy to finalize conflict agreements, Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said. The purpose of any meeting would be to agree on the conflict's final arrangements.

item.title: Iran Says Reopening Strait of Hormuz 'Not Possible' Under US Naval Blockade
item.description: Iran has stated it cannot reopen the Strait of Hormuz under a US naval blockade. The statement comes after the US-Iran conflict began on February 28. US-Iran war broke out on February 28.

item.title: To Iran, Trump Blinked First by Extending the Cease-Fire
item.description: Iran's leaders believe they can outlast President Trump in a standoff, which could devastate average Iranians economically. Iran’s leaders believe that they can withstand an enduring standoff longer than President Trump.
